title: India, Canada sign $2.6 billion uranium deal, target $50 billion trade by 2030 with CEPA push
description: India and Canada have signed significant agreements on uranium and critical minerals. Both nations aim to boost bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. This partnership includes cooperation in defense, technology, and renewable energy. The leaders finalized terms for a comprehensive economic partnership agreement. This move signifies a reset in relations and unlocks new opportunities for both countries.
